agent.root_goal: Produce a bilingual (EN/KR) developer style guide for 'Cheonma', an internal payments platform, in two independently-owned parts. Part A (platform standards team): research Cheonma's legacy services and write the canonical terminology standard - endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, and casing rules. Cheonma's conventions are idiosyncratic house style discovered from its legacy code, not industry defaults; they are unknown until Part A publishes them. Part B (developer relations team): write the worked integration tutorial with runnable code samples for the checkout and refund flows. Company handbook rule, applying to the whole guide: documentation ships only if every identifier, endpoint name, and error code appearing anywhere in the guide matches the published canonical standard exactly; term drift between parts is a shipping defect.

action.reasoning: The task requires producing two distinct parts of a developer style guide with a strict dependency: Part B (tutorial) must use the terminology defined in Part A (standards). Since Part A's output is unknown and required for Part B to avoid 'term drift', this is a sequential decomposition. I will spawn Part A to run first, then spawn Part B conditioned on Part A's completion. I will take the self_role of integrating the two parts into the final bilingual guide document.

subtasks.goal: Research Cheonma's legacy services and write the canonical terminology standard.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask acts as the Platform Standards Team. It must discover the idiosyncratic house style of Cheonma (endpoint nouns, error codes, casing) from legacy code context. This output is the single source of truth for terminology, preventing term drift in the final guide.

outputs.description: A JSON or structured text document defining the canonical endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, and casing rules for Cheonma, in both EN and KR.

subtasks.goal: Write the worked integration tutorial with runnable code samples for checkout and refund flows.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask acts as the Developer Relations Team. It must write the tutorial using the exact terminology defined in root.1/canonical_terminology_standard. It cannot proceed until the standard is published to ensure compliance with the company handbook rule against term drift.

outputs.description: A bilingual (EN/KR) tutorial document with runnable code samples for checkout and refund flows, strictly adhering to the canonical terminology standard.

self_role.goal: Integrate the terminology standard and integration tutorial into the final bilingual developer style guide.

outputs.description: The final, unified bilingual (EN/KR) developer style guide for Cheonma, combining Part A and Part B.
